frameworks.name: predictive Adaptive Resonance Theory (pART)
frameworks.one_line: unified prefrontal-cortex model integrating cognition, emotion, and goal-directed action

frameworks.name: Adaptive Resonance Theory
frameworks.one_line: earlier ART foundation extended with predictive prefrontal mechanisms

frameworks.name: executive-function theory
frameworks.one_line: frames prefrontal role in flexible behavior and inhibitory control

core_question: How does prefrontal cortex coordinate with multiple brain regions to produce executive control over cognition, emotion, and action?
key_assumptions: prefrontal cortex acts via distributed interactions across orbitofrontal, ventrolateral, and dorsolateral subregions
executive functions require coupling with inferotemporal, perirhinal, parahippocampal, hippocampus, amygdala, basal ganglia, hypothalamus, and visual cortex
working memory supports contextually appropriate behavior
reflexive sensory-driven responses must be suppressed for adaptive behavior
interpretations: pART unifies prefrontal computations including desirability, availability, credit assignment, category learning, and feature-based attention
value of visual objects/events and prediction of consequences emerge from prefrontal-temporal-limbic interactions
out_of_scope_note: Textbook chapter (theory synthesis, not empirical paper); abstract describes a unified model without manipulations or empirical findings.
